# trusin
> trusin is an open-source, self-hosted webhook relay for reliable delivery operations.

trusin receives webhook events, stores their history in Postgres, queues delivery with Redis, routes events to targets, retries retryable failures, and provides a dashboard, CLI/TUI, and MCP server.

## Primary links
- [Website](https://trusin.my.id/): Product overview and webhook relay guide.
- [Documentation](https://docs.trusin.my.id/docs/intro): Product and deployment documentation.
- [Webhook relay guide](https://trusin.my.id/webhook-relay): What a webhook relay is and when to use one.
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/adityaputra11/terusin): Source code, releases, and issue tracker.
- [Dashboard](https://app.trusin.my.id/): Hosted application.

## Documentation
- [Architecture](https://docs.trusin.my.id/docs/concepts/architecture): trusin documentation: Architecture.
- [Reliability model](https://docs.trusin.my.id/docs/concepts/reliability): trusin documentation: Reliability model.
- [Authentication and RBAC](https://docs.trusin.my.id/docs/guides/authentication): trusin documentation: Authentication and RBAC.
- [CLI and MCP](https://docs.trusin.my.id/docs/guides/cli-and-mcp): trusin documentation: CLI and MCP.
- [Local development](https://docs.trusin.my.id/docs/guides/local-development): trusin documentation: Local development.
- [Receiving and forwarding webhooks](https://docs.trusin.my.id/docs/guides/webhooks): trusin documentation: Receiving and forwarding webhooks.
- [Welcome](https://docs.trusin.my.id/docs/intro): trusin documentation: trusin.
- [Self-hosted webhook infrastructure](https://docs.trusin.my.id/docs/learn/self-hosted-infrastructure): Run trusin on infrastructure you control with Postgres, Redis, HTTPS, backups, and operational visibility.
- [Webhook delivery](https://docs.trusin.my.id/docs/learn/webhook-delivery): How webhook delivery works in trusin: receive an event, persist it, queue it, deliver it, and record the result.
- [Webhook relay vs. queues](https://docs.trusin.my.id/docs/learn/webhook-relay-vs-queues): Understand the difference between a webhook relay and a queue, and why reliable webhook delivery needs both.
- [Webhook retries](https://docs.trusin.my.id/docs/learn/webhook-retries): Learn how trusin retries failed webhooks, what retryable failures mean, and how to design idempotent receivers.
- [Production deployment](https://docs.trusin.my.id/docs/operations/deployment): trusin documentation: Production deployment.
- [Testing](https://docs.trusin.my.id/docs/operations/testing): trusin documentation: Testing.
- [Troubleshooting](https://docs.trusin.my.id/docs/operations/troubleshooting): trusin documentation: Troubleshooting.
- [API reference](https://docs.trusin.my.id/docs/reference/api): trusin documentation: API reference.
- [Configuration](https://docs.trusin.my.id/docs/reference/configuration): trusin documentation: Configuration.

## AI and integration notes
- Use API tokens for CLI and MCP access; never request or expose a dashboard password.
- The bundled MCP server runs locally over stdio and uses TERUSIN_TOKEN.
- Webhook delivery is at-least-once; target handlers should be idempotent.
- For complete documentation content, read https://trusin.my.id/llms-full.txt.
